name: Data Storage Systems
tagline: Persistent storage for documents, software, and datasets
problem:
Civilization fails when knowledge cannot persist. Memory inside a running computer is not enough: you need durable storage for documents, software, and measurements.

Storage systems solve persistence with tradeoffs: cost, reliability, speed, and power. The hard part is not writing bits; it is integrity over time and repairability.
overview:
Storage spans multiple tiers: paper and print, magnetic media, solid-state flash, and redundant distributed storage. Each tier has different failure modes: bit rot, mechanical wear, radiation, humidity, and human error.

For The Codex, storage is a survival technology: keep critical references in multiple formats, with checksums and redundancy, and test restores regularly.

principles.name: Redundancy
principles.explanation: Multiple copies and parity prevent single failures from destroying knowledge.

principles.name: Checksums and integrity
principles.explanation: Hashing detects corruption; regular verification is required.

principles.name: Lifecycle and failure modes
principles.explanation: Media wear and environmental stress determine refresh schedules.

rawMaterials.name: Electric power
rawMaterials.purpose: Energy for computation, storage, and networking
earthLocations: Electrical grids globally; generators in remote sites
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Solar + batteries is the simplest early-space pathway; plan for tight energy budgets and thermal rejection.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Stable voltage regulation, grounding, and basic power-quality control.

rawMaterials.name: Semiconductor devices
rawMaterials.purpose: Transistors and ICs that perform switching, storage, and signal conditioning
earthLocations: Global semiconductor supply chains; packaged IC distributors
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Early colonies import packaged devices; long-term local manufacture requires clean chemistry and crystal growth capabilities.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: ESD-safe handling; correct packaging, heat sinking, and derating for temperature and radiation.

rawMaterials.name: Documentation and standards
rawMaterials.purpose: Make designs reproducible and debuggable across teams and time
earthLocations: Libraries, universities, standards bodies, archived web
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Mirror core references offline; maintain printed and digital copies with checksums and redundancy.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Versioning, change control, and clear measurement units and assumptions.

rawMaterials.name: Magnetic materials and precision parts
rawMaterials.purpose: Disk/tape media and mechanical assemblies (where used)
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Favor solid-state and printed backups early; mechanical storage is maintenance-heavy off-world.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Clean assembly and alignment; environmental sealing.

rawMaterials.name: Printed references
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Print key manuals; store in controlled environments; keep redundancy.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Indexing, revision control, and archive discipline.

buildSteps.title: Choose redundancy strategy
buildSteps.description: Define what must survive; decide copy count and locations; use offline backups.
buildSteps.warningNote: If you have only one copy, you do not have the data.

buildSteps.title: Define integrity checks
buildSteps.description: Use checksums and periodic verification; record hash manifests.

buildSteps.title: Test restores
buildSteps.description: Practice restoring from backup; document steps and dependencies.
buildSteps.warningNote: Backups that cannot be restored are theater.

buildSteps.title: Refresh media on schedule
buildSteps.description: Rotate and migrate data before end-of-life; store spares.
buildSteps.warningNote: Old media fails at the worst time.

history.event: Paper archives and microfilm scale knowledge storage.
Magnetic tape and disks dominate computing storage.
Flash and distributed storage become standard.

impact:
Reliable storage is the difference between accumulated capability and repeated rediscovery. It enables science, engineering, governance, and long-term planning.

In the Codex, storage sits near the base of modern computing and of any resilience strategy for collapse recovery or space settlement.

sources.title: Tanenbaum & Bos — Modern Operating Systems (storage/filesystems context)
